metrics.quality_rate: Percentage of records where ticket_reviews.ai_approved=true OR change_classification is in the 'Good' group (PERFECT_MATCH, STYLISTIC_EDIT, STRUCTURAL_FIX, TONAL_MISALIGNMENT). Note: ai_approved lives in ticket_reviews table, NOT in ai_human_comparison. Use the VIEW ai_comparison_with_reviews or JOIN. Formula: COUNT(good) / COUNT(total) * 100
metrics.quality_score: Penalty-based score per record: PERFECT_MATCH=100, STYLISTIC_EDIT=98, STRUCTURAL_FIX=95, TONAL_MISALIGNMENT=90, CONFUSING_VERBOSITY=85, MINOR_INFO_GAP=80, MAJOR_FUNCTIONAL_OMISSION=50, CRITICAL_FACT_ERROR=0. Average across records gives the overall score.
metrics.approval_rate: Percentage of records where ticket_reviews.ai_approved=true out of all reviewed records. ai_approved is in ticket_reviews, not ai_human_comparison.
metrics.changed_rate_legacy: Legacy v3.x metric: percentage of records where changed=true (human edited AI output)

business_rules.quality_priority: If ticket_reviews.ai_approved=true, the record counts as 'Quality' regardless of change_classification value. IMPORTANT: ai_approved is in ticket_reviews table, NOT ai_human_comparison. Use JOIN or the VIEW ai_comparison_with_reviews.
business_rules.excluded_classifications: EXCL_WORKFLOW_SHIFT, EXCL_DATA_DISCREPANCY, HUMAN_INCOMPLETE are excluded from quality scoring — do NOT include them in quality calculations
business_rules.classification_case: All change_classification values are UPPERCASE with underscores: PERFECT_MATCH, STYLISTIC_EDIT, etc. Never lowercase.
business_rules.qualified_agents: Only specific agents (defined in the application) can perform ticket reviews. Their emails are stored in the email column.
business_rules.date_timezone: All timestamps are in UTC. For display, use AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Jerusalem' to convert to Israel local time.

common_gotchas: action_analysis_verification in ticket_reviews is JSONB — use ->>'key' for text access, ->'key' for JSON object access
ai_human_comparison.change_classification can be NULL — always filter with IS NOT NULL when aggregating classifications
ticket_reviews.ai_approved can be NULL (not yet reviewed), true, or false — use COALESCE or IS TRUE / IS NOT TRUE filters. This column is in ticket_reviews, not ai_human_comparison!
Use ROUND() for all decimal/percentage results for readability
Always use LIMIT 50 by default for large result sets unless user asks for all
The VIEW ai_comparison_with_reviews provides a pre-joined view of ai_human_comparison LEFT JOIN ticket_reviews
Dates are timestamptz — use AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Jerusalem' for local time display in Israel
For quality scoring, always exclude records where change_classification starts with 'EXCL_' or equals 'HUMAN_INCOMPLETE'
When calculating percentages, use NULLIF(denominator, 0) to avoid division by zero
request_subtype contains the category name — this is the main grouping field for category analysis
prompt_version contains version identifiers like 'v3', 'v4' etc. — used for version comparison
